title: Amid beautiful weather, coastal flooding could impact Boston
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Local News Humidity and storms are expected to return later this week.
Morrissey Boulevard in Dorchester is often at risk of flooding.
Barry Chin/Boston Globe People in Boston and other coastal areas of Massachusetts should be wary of potential coastal flooding Tuesday, according to weather experts.

title: 22-year-old dies after being pulled from Cape Cod pond
author: John Waller
bullets: Local News The incident happened Monday evening.

A 22-year-old man died Monday after being pulled from a Cape Cod pond.

title: CVS Pharmacy is opening a new store in Nubian Square
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Local News After Walgreens closed multiple stores in Roxbury, it's gone without a chain pharmacy for years.
The CVS logo is displayed on a pharmacy and retail location, Wednesday, Aug.

title: Boston 25 News anchor announces her departure from the station
author: John Waller
bullets: Media "It has been an honor to serve this community and tell the stories that matter most to New England.

" By John Waller June 16, 2026 | 11:31 AM 1 minute to read Boston 25 News anchor and reporter Rachel Keller announced on social media Tuesday morning that she’ll be leaving the station at the end of next week.
“It has been an honor to serve this community and tell the stories that matter most to New England,” she said in the post.

title: Watch: Bagpipes and bucket drums make a culture connection at Faneuil Hall
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: World Cup “One lone piper from Scotland and Boston’s finest bucket-drum maestro somehow created the collaboration nobody knew they needed.
” World Cup: If there’s one thing we’ve learned from the World Cup this year, it’s that it brings people together.
Like, for instance, the entire town of Lawrence, Kansas turning overnight into Algerian soccer fans, as documented in a widely circulated post by former congressman Adam Kinzinger.

title: A new ice cream and coffee shop just opened in Eastie — inside a caboose
author: Madison Lucchesi
bullets: Restaurants The Blue Caboose is serving cold brew, ice cream, and sundaes from a historic caboose in East Boston.
Madison Lucchesi ShareSend this article to your social connections.
For years, East Boston residents walking along the Mary Ellen Welch Greenway passed the same sight: a bright blue train car sitting empty and covered in graffiti.

title: R.I. man allegedly used more than 900 online accounts to stalk Boston woman
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Crime “[The defendant] also created a profile pretending to be the victim herself to gather personal information about her from a friend,” the DA said.
A Rhode Island man was arraigned in Boston last week for harassing a Boston women, allegedly creating more than 900 different social media accounts to stalk her, prosecutors announced.
Space Vixaisak, 23, is charged with one count of stalking and one count of violating a harassment prevention order, the office of Suffolk County District Attorney Kevin Hayden said.
